popularStory.description: Charles d'Anjou (21 mars 1227 – 7 janvier 1285), roi de Naples et de Sicile (1266-1285), est le dernier fils du roi de France Louis VIII et de Blanche de Castille. Comte d’Anjou et du Maine, il devien …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile於1227年3月21日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲時，他的父親Louis VIII Roi de France39歲，母親Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia39歲。他於1268年11月18日與Marguerite de Bourgogne結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1285年1月7日在義大利普格利亞霍治亞享年57歲，埋葬在義大利坎帕尼亞拿坡里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile于1227年3月21日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲时，他的父亲Louis VIII Roi de France39岁，母亲Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia39岁。他于1268年11月18日与Marguerite de Bourgogne结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1285年1月7日在意大利普格利亞霍治亞享年57岁，埋葬在意大利坎帕尼亞拿坡里。

nameMeanings.name: d'Anjou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France (see Langevin ).

variants.name: Langevin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Henri, Jean-Louis, Pierre, Adelard, Emile, Gaston, Leodore, Lucien, Marcel.

French: habitational name, with fused definite article l', for an Angevin, i.e. for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France that was ruled by a count as an independent territory from the 10th century. The province became a property of the English Crown for fifty years at the end of the 12th century. Compare Longway and Lonsway .

History: Mathurin Langevin dit Lacroix from Le Lude in Sarthe, France, married Marie-Thérèse Martin in Quebec City, QC, in 1674. Michel Langevin from Paris, France, married Marguerite Guertin in Contrecoeur, QC, in 1714. Jean Bergevin dit Langevin from Angers in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie Piton in Quebec City, QC, in 1668. Charles Citoleux/Sitoleux dit Langevin from Chambellay in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie-Louise Harel in Montreal, QC, in 1739.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile was born on 21 March 1227, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, his father, Louis VIII Roi de France, was 39 and his mother, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, was 39. He married Marguerite de Bourgogne on 18 November 1268. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 7 January 1285, in Foggia, Puglia, Italy, at the age of 57, and was buried in Naples, Campania, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile est né le 21 mars 1227, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, son père, Louis VIII Roi de France, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite de Bourgogne le 18 novembre 1268. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 7 janvier 1285, à Foggia, Puglia, Italie, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Napoli, Campania, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile am 21. März 1227 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Louis VIII Roi de France 39 und seine Mutter Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia 39. Er hat am 18. November 1268 Marguerite de Bourgogne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 7. Januar 1285 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Foggia, Puglia, Italien und wurde in Napoli, Campania, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile è nato il il 21 marzo 1227 nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Louis VIII Roi de France, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite de Bourgogne il 18 novembre 1268. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 7 gennaio 1285, nel Foggia, Puglia, Italia, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Napoli, Campania, Italia.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicileが1227年3月21日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで生まれた時，父Louis VIII Roi de Franceは39歳，母Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Franciaは39歳であった。1268年11月18日にMarguerite de Bourgogneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1285年1月7日にイタリアプウグリアフォグジアで57歳で死去し，イタリアカンプアニアナポリにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile(이)가 1227년 3월 21일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Louis VIII Roi de France(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1268년 11월 18일 Marguerite de Bourgogne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1285년 1월 7일 이탈리아 푸그리야 포지아에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 캠파니아 나폴리에 묻혔다.

mn.21 March 1227–07 January 1285: 1227 оны Гуравдугаар сарын 21–1285 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 7

pt.21 March 1227–07 January 1285: 21 de março de 1227–7 de janeiro de 1285
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile nasceu em 21 de março de 1227, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Louis VIII Roi de France, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite de Bourgogne em 18 de novembro de 1268. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 7 de janeiro de 1285, em Foggia, Puglia, Itália, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Napoli, Campania, Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile родился 21 марта 1227 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Louis VIII Roi de France было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite de Bourgogne 18 ноября 1268 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 7 января 1285 г. в местности фогя, пугля, Италия в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности наполи, кампаня, Италия.

es.21 March 1227–07 January 1285: 21 de marzo de 1227–7 de enero de 1285
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles I d'Anjou Roi de Naples et de Sicile nació el 21 de marzo de 1227, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Louis VIII Roi de France, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Marguerite de Bourgogne el 18 de noviembre de 1268. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 7 de enero de 1285, en Foggia, Puglia, Italia, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Napoli, Campania, Italia.
